details.text: Derivative: سَبَّحَ comes from the word سِباحَة (Sibaahah) which means literally to swim or to float. تَسْبِيح (Tasbeeh) means to make something swim or float, in the case where floating means that is stays in its place and does not go down. The تَسْبِيح (Tasbeeh) of Allah is to say things about Allah that befit Him, to declare Allah’s perfection, greatness, mercy, creative power and what is similar, maintaining that whilst not saying anything that allows that status to come down. The Subhaan (سُبْحان) of Allah is that we refuse to say anything about Allah that is beneath Him or not fitting or becoming to be ascribed to Him. This is the response of the Qur’an when people make unbecoming statements concerning Allah or attribute to Him that which is not befitting. It represents a constant declaration of Allah’s perfection and free of any flaw. تَسْبِيح (Tasbeeh) relates to us maintaining the grandeur of Allah as we should.

Derivative: مَقْتًا – مَقْت in origin refers to an abhorrent pre-Islamic practise where a son would marry his stepmothers after his father passed away. It is used to describe something that is viewed to be abhorrent, hateful and repulsive.

Derivative: مَرْصُوصٌ – Means to be pressed together. Here in reference to a building or built structure it refers to all the bricks being held firmly together where the bricks are the Muslims fighting in rows in the way of Allah.

Derivative: أَحْمَدُ (Ahmad) can be an Ism Tafdeel (اِسْم التَّفْضِيل) from حَامِد (Haamid) which means to be someone who praises more than others. أَحْمَدُ (Ahmad) can also come from the meaning of مَحْمُود (Mahmood) which means that he is praised more than others. Both these meanings are embedded in the word أَحْمَدُ (Ahmad).
- بَيْنَ يَدَيَّ – Literally “Between my hands” is an expression to mean “right in front of or before me” and implies direct access.
Derivative: يُطْفِئُوا from أَطْفَأَ – يُطْفِئٌ means to put out or extinguish (the light of Allah).
Derivative: الْكَافِرُونَ from كُفْر (Kufr) which carries the meaning of burying something. When you bury something, you are placing it in the dark.
- In this verse Allah states that He will perfect His light which those who bury the light or truth (الْكَافِرُونَ) want to extinguish.
- “The light of Allah” refers to any manifestation of Islam and Allah’s divine guidance.
